title: Font engine unification for scene + demo parity
description: Unify font rendering/profile logic into one Python engine and make InfoScene match font showcase visuals.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Started implementation: introducing canonical font profiles and shared renderer, then migrating scene + showcase paths and tests.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Implemented canonical font profiles + shared renderer and migrated scene/info + showcase to consume shared profile data. Automated tests passing; awaiting manual device parity confirmation for each font in info scene.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Continuing in-progress: replacing scene text rasterization internals with single canonical glyph-atlas renderer path to eliminate blur/noise divergence from validated font demo. Acceptance target: info scene text visually matches font showcase on-device.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Implemented renderer rewrite: scene text now uses cached per-glyph atlas composition (high-res glyph -> nearest downsample -> threshold -> compose) matching the font showcase method. Targeted font/info/scene/cli tests are passing; proceeding to real-device visual verification.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: User confirmed visual fix on real device and Micro5 switch works. Story criteria met for unified renderer parity.
